behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local backoff math/state (attempt count -> delay with cap). No real I/O is required. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is only about delay progression/cap, not transport, timers, or external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Test the backoff policy object/function directly with consecutive failure counts and max cap settings; assert delay sequence (e.g., 100, 200, 400, ... capped).

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at a module handoff: response classification + retry/backoff module coordination. In-process stub responses are sufficient. Rejected System because behavior does not require real network/HTTP stack; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry orchestrator with a stub transport returning a synthetic 429/rate-limit payload/headers; assert it is classified as rate-limit and computed wait matches policy.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Needs orchestration across retry loop, timer/scheduler, and request sender. Real OS/network not required; fake clock + stub transport can expose resend/return path bugs. Rejected System because behavior text is about library control flow, not real HTTP conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Using fake timers and a stub transport that first returns rate-limit then success, call `call()` once and assert: second send occurs after computed delay, payload/request identity is preserved, and resolved result equals successful response.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Type of surfaced error depends on retry-loop exhaustion path plus error-mapping module, not just a pure function. Real infra is unnecessary with deterministic stub failures. Rejected System since no real network/process behavior is needed; rejected Agentic/Workflow as non-LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to always return rate-limit; configure max retries; assert final rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` instance/type and not generic Error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module state-machine termination invariant (retry counter + quota tracking + scheduler). In-process test infra with fake clock can catch infinite-loop/regression failures. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real OS/network effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run `call()` with transport that never succeeds under fake timers; advance time through all scheduled waits; assert finite number of attempts, terminal rejection reached, and no further retries scheduled afterward.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is shared in-process state across concurrent callers (module interaction/race), not a single-function rule. No real external infra is required; deterministic local concurrency tests can reproduce. Rejected System because behavior explicitly scopes to 'same process'.
behaviors.test_description: Create one client instance with shared quota bucket and launch multiple concurrent `call()` invocations via stub transport; assert aggregate consumption follows one common quota counter and callers observe throttling based on shared state.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Ordering guarantee is a queueing/orchestration property across scheduler + request queue + dispatcher modules. Pure unit tests of queue helpers can miss real handoff bugs. Rejected System because real network/OS is not required to validate FIFO dispatch semantics.
behaviors.test_description: With quota constrained and fake timers, enqueue multiple calls quickly; use stub transport to record dispatch order; assert send order matches submission order under waiting/retry conditions.
